entities.overall_summary: Sutton Place is my favorite hotel in Chicago Magnificent Mile area. The location is great, the staff is friendly, and the room is clean. However, the location is a little off the beaten path, and there are a lot of places to eat and drink in the area. I have never felt the hotel deserved a "choice" award, this time they don't even deserve a star.

parents.summary: Bathroom & Sanitary Area: The bathroom was spacious and luxuriously appointed with a soaker tub and separate shower. Building Infrastructure: This high-rise hotel has two elevators and one elevator was not working, therefore wait times getting an elevator was longer than usual in the lobby and from your floor level. Air Conditioning & Ventilation: I am a big fan a larger, chain hotels but overall, I can recommend Sutton Place Chicago. Noise & Environmental Comfort: Located in a lively neighborhood with great restaurants and bars and is only a short walk to Miracle Mile shopping and attractions. Interior Design & Ambience: Check in was fast, bellman friendly and we got a great room on 19th floor which was spacious, clean and well provided for in terms of towels, amenities and furniture. The hotel bar, The Wiskey has a great selection of cocktails and wines and the atmosphere is great with a mixed crowd of young and old alike, not atall stuffy.

summaries.neutral: We had just checked into the Grand, with minor problems, we always request adjoining rooms and that seems to be a small problem for this hotel, but, the fire alarm and sprinkler system had gone off on our floor and to be precise in the room right next to mine and my sisters, we waited patiently while the staff was in the room where the sprinkler originally went off, the carpeting in our rooms were soaked as was some of our luggage and kids toys, etc. it seems to me that someone would really have to be a bad cook to set off the fire alarm with a microwave but they claim thats there number 1 problem. I believe they recieved a 500 dollar fine and the major cause of fire alarm emergencies would be improper use of the microwave ovens. not to mention the complimentary FIRE ALARM EVACUATION AT 10:30 p.m., looks like another reoccurring problem the "GRAND" has put aside to fix.

parents.summary: Entertainment & Recreation: In the game room four of the game either did not work or ate your money which meant going down to front desk to get a credit to your room and $1 for a game of Ms. pacman is robbery. ), you can't connect DVD or video games console to the TV for the rare moment you want to stay in and watch movies, their restaurant only serves breakfast, the rooms have 2 queen sized HARD beds (king size is not an option). Transportation & Parking: We could usually park right in the front parking lot; but there is also a ramp garage right next to the hotel that always had plenty of spots. Great parking too, a huge parking garage just steps away from front door, something many don't have. The absolute best thing about the Grand is the parking garage, which has quite a lot of space, and is perfect, since finding parking in Ocean City, without having to walk a few blocks and crossing the busy highway isn't exactly easy. We were even able to enjoy the beach the following day during our checkout date since we didn't have to move our car out of the parking garage till 2pm. parents.summary: Entertainment & Recreation: There is a kitchenette in the living room and a flat screen tv in the kitchenette. In-room Utilities: The room came equipped with everything we needed to have an enjoyable dinner in front of the fireplace (dishes, utensils, wine glasses, etc--they even have microwave, refrigerator, and two burner stove). Absolutely gorgeous suite with entrance including closet and a wonderfully handy little table to drop your keys, maps, change etc, then a well equipped kitchenette with microwave, refrigerator, stove, etc. The full kitchenette with refrigerator, stove and sink was great - we could not remember staying EVER at any hotel that included REAL glasses (water, cocktail AND wine glasses). As a coffee addict, I especially appreciated a large, 12 cup coffee maker supplied with a more than acceptable blend--no tiny pot dribbling out foul brown water like most hotels. Transportation & Parking: The staff are wonderful - they greet you by name once you've registered, the valet parking is quick and efficient, the registration staff lovely. The staff is helpful and respectful, especially with the valet parking. The staff was incredibly gracious, and we felt very pampered and taken care of - from the valet parking to the moment we left. Laundry & Utility Services: Even tried to make up for past troubles (lost my laundry for a day) by giving me a special edible treat, which turned out to be spoiled! While they do way more than I would expect (like washing the dishes we used from the kitchenette), it was a constant battle to keep our toilet paper and tissues replenished. Wifi & Connectivity: Wifi & Connectivity (AM_WIFI) is one of the most important aspects of a hotel.

summaries.neutral: That said, we opened all three windows and cooled the upper room down that way; and the rest of the staff were fantastic, the hotel is very cleverly converted to combine an old building with modern touches, and the location is ideal. There is no view, our windows opened on the wall of the adjacent building; this is not a disadvantage in the winter when the sun raises late and guarantees a total silence during night; you may sleep there with open windows. entities.overall_summary: FACILITY: The hotel is in a good location for good restaurants nearby head towards Rue Mouffetard and the hotel is very clean with lovely furniture and tastefully decorated. If you don't mind handing over security of your items to some hired help who was hogging the guest's computer (by the way they charge for wifi which is really nickel and diming guests) by all means stay here. They seemed to want to lock the front door as early as they could but always opened it promptly. AMENITY: We enjoyed having the internet and two TV channels in English. We had a TV which did offer a couple of English stations and a radio in the room as well. 3. Transportation & Parking: The hotel manager was so heplful to confirm our shuttle booking to the airport, he did not mind to use his own cell phone and spoke French to clarify the pick up time. After waiting for around 30 minutes after the shuttle was due the man on reception phoned the shuttle operators & we were informed that there has been a mistake & there would be a further delay before the shuttle would arrive but the timing could not be guaranteed. SERVICE: The staff is very friendly and helpful. The rooms are small, but spotlessly clean and the reception staff are very helpful. EXPERIENCE: Emotional Experience: Hotel review: This was poor and the stress this problem caused detracted from what was an enjoyable weekend. Subjective Safety Feeling: Being only four women we felt very safe around the hotel area and the restaurants we visited were about five minutes walk. BRANDING: Luxury & Premium Perception: This place is a 1 star compared to the Acte V and just shows how the good Trip Advisor system can be distorted wrongly and good hotels (and ultimately travellers pay). It is very reasonable for a 3 star hotel ( we paid about £340 for 3 nights in a double room B and B . It was great 3 star, modern refurb, friendly staff, great bar and lounge area, large well furnished rooms, aircdon, flat screen tv, little balconies, quiet, great location. Very few 3-star hotels have air conditioning (including this one). Brand Reputation & Reliability: The rooms are smallish and the breakfast is not worth the $12 price.
